definition: minimum insulation resistance between terminals of a fixed capacitor, determined according to IEC 60384-1(4.5) (1984) at reference conditions

definition_ml.en: minimum insulation resistance between terminals of a fixed capacitor, determined according to IEC 60384-1(4.5) (1984) at reference conditions

remark: 1. The insulation resistance is measured after a voltage has been applied for 1 min +/- 5 s. The relationship between this voltage and the rated voltage (dc) is prescribed in IEC 60384-1 (Amd2)(4.5.2) (1987).|, voltage rating of capacitor................measuring voltage| U_R} or U_C} < 10 V.................................U_R} or U_C} +/-10%|10 V <= U_R} or U_C} < 100 V ..............10 +/- 1 V|100 V <= U_R} or U_C} < 500 V.............100 +/-15 V| 500 V <= U_R} or U_C}............................500 +/-50 V|| 2. AAE063-005 is prescribed by IEC for specific capacitor types. See also AAE069-005
